frames.title: The Fire Had Already Burned Out

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: By the time the brothers reached Yingchuan, half the sky had already burned red. The great battle was over. Only blackened camps and bodies remained.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: The routed rebels had not even finished running when a red-banner force cut across them. The man leading it was Cao Cao, the sort of man who seemed born to close off a battlefield.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Huangfu Song gave them only one order: Zhang Liang and Zhang Bao would likely flee to Guangzong. Go back at once and help Lu Zhi.

frames.title: Lu Zhi in a Prison Cart

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: On the road back, a prison cart came toward them. Inside it sat Lu Zhi.

frames.title: Zhang Fei Wants to Cut This Rage Loose

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Lu Zhi explained everything calmly. Zhang Fei reached for his blade at once, but Liu Bei forced that rage back down.

frames.title: They Had Barely Swallowed It When Another Defeat Appeared

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Less than two days later, another disaster broke out behind the hills. From the ridge, the brothers saw Yellow Turban pursuers flooding over a beaten Han force.

frames.title: Liu Bei Reads the Field Before He Moves

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Liu Bei did not waste words. He read the direction of pursuit and retreat in a glance, and Guan Yu and Zhang Fei understood the plan from that alone.

frames.title: The Brothers Split Open the Collapse

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: The three brothers struck like three blades and carved a path through the rout.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Their ability was visible at last. The road ahead, though, was no less dark.
